titlestmt.titleproper: Photographs and postcards of women collection7678
titlestmt.author: Brenda Marston

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2019-11-15 00:15:02 +0000.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Photographs and postcards of women collection
did.abstract: A collection of photographs and postcards from various sources, primarily of unidentified women by unidentified photographers that show women in various homosocial poses or in gender transgressive dress or behavior. Some images may be of sisters, relatives, or female friends; some are explicitly lesbian in nature. Together, they give visual documentation of women's relationships, body language, and dress. Specific descriptions follow.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Peter MartinezAndrea Hektor, April 2004Sarah Keen, January 2009

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Photographs and postcards of gender collection, #7717.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Photographs and postcards of women collection, #7678. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: A collection of photographs and postcards from various sources, primarily of unidentified women by unidentified photographers that show women in various homosocial poses or in gender transgressive dress or behavior. Some images may be of sisters, relatives, or female friends; some are explicitly lesbian in nature. Together, they give visual documentation of women's relationships, body language, and dress. Specific descriptions follow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Portrait from the turn of the 20th century of 2 women, one with her hand on the other's shoulder. The women's identities and relationship are unknown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: A group of 10 postcards and 3 photographs, circa 1900-1950, on a variety of themes, all unidentified, and not necessarily related to each other. Two are of solitary women, one of which is a woman sitting on a log in a forested area, wearing a long skirt, cardigan, and blouse with man's tie. A few photos show groups of women wearing ornate hats. One may be a valentine. Several look like family pictures, of sisters, or mother and daughter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: German postcard, two women on porch, two women within arms of third.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Typically formatted American postcard of the early 20th century, showing two young women sitting on a rail of a deck with their arms around each other. The text reads: "She squeezed her and kissed her without any joy, ..'It's the masculine fervor you lack,' said she, ''Tis easy to tell you are far from a boy, By the soft-pedal tone of the smack, you see." Formerly #7646.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Three nude or semi-nude images of women together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Photo album containing 27 photos, plus 30 loose photos, creator unknown. Advertised on eBay as "Lesbian Interest, Old Photo Album All Women," the album is indeed entirely photographs of girls and women, both young and old, dating from circa 1910-1957. Their relationships are unknown. Ten photos are of two women together. Others are possibly mothers and daughters, grandmothers and daughters, and groups of friends, as well as photos of individuals. They are mostly informal shots; just a few are studio portraits. One is marked "Black's Studio, Wewoka, Okla." Names are written on the back of only a few photographs. They are: Mary L. Irwin, Jan Rorke?, and Mrs. Brenner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Two women looking at each other, circa 1930s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Photographic postcard, circa 1905, showing a woman in man's clothing sitting on a porch rocker with her arm around another woman in more traditional female clothing. On the verso is written "Dear friend: ...I make a pretty good looking boy don't I?...Your friend Ella."

controlaccess.subject: Women -- United States.
Transsexuals -- Travel -- Italy.
Lesbians -- United States -- Caricatures and cartoons.
Lesbians -- Germany -- Caricatures and cartoons.
Female friendship.

did.unittitle: A group of 10 postcards and 3 photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 images on a variety of themes, all unidentified, and not necessarily related to each other. Two are of solitary women, one of which is a woman sitting on a log in a forested area, wearing a long skirt, cardigan, and blouse with man's tie. A few photos show groups of women wearing ornate hats. One may be a valentine. Several look like family pictures, of sisters, or mother and daughter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: German postcard, two women on porch, two women within arms of third.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcard showing two young women sitting on a rail of a deck with their arms around each other. The text reads: "She squeezed her and kissed her without any joy, ..'It's the masculine fervor you lack,' said she, ''Tis easy to tell you are far from a boy, By the soft-pedal tone of the smack, you see." Formerly #7646

did.unittitle: Three nude or semi-nude images of women together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertised on eBay as "Lesbian Interest, Old Photo Album All Women," the album is indeed entirely photographs of girls and women, both young and old, dating from circa 1910-1957. Their relationships are unknown. Ten photos are of two women together. Others are possibly mothers and daughters, grandmothers and daughters, and groups of friends, as well as photos of individuals. They are mostly informal shots; just a few are studio portraits. One is marked "Black's Studio, Wewoka, Okla." Names are written on the back of only a few photographs. They are: Mary L. Irwin, Jan Rorke?, and Mrs. Brenner.

did.unittitle: Photo album containing 27 photos, corresponding to the photos in Box 1 Folder 5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small photo of 2 women standing next to each other, hugging and looking at each other. Described on eBay as "GIRLFRIENDS Hug and LOOK in Eyes LESBIAN Int."

did.unittitle: Photographic postcard showing a woman in man's clothing sitting on a porch rocker with her arm around another woman in more traditional female clothing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On the verso is written: "Dear friend: Will send you a card. Do you know either one in the picture? I make a pretty good looking boy don't I? The other is a girl friend of mine. We had them taken on our porch just for the fun of it. Your friend Ella." Above the inscription is written: "Did you ever see the suit before is it C.B. they fit me pretty good."
